pattern: Case analysis on a fixed 8x8 board: check whether the rook or bishop already attacks the queen directly (possibly blocked by the other piece), returning 1 if so and 2 otherwise.
hints: If the rook shares a row or column with the queen, it attacks in 1 move unless the bishop lies exactly between them on that line.
If the bishop shares a diagonal with the queen, it attacks in 1 move unless the rook lies exactly between them on that diagonal.
If neither piece can reach the queen directly, 2 moves always suffice — the rook can always reach the queen in at most 2 rook moves.

edge_inputs.description: Rook on same row as queen, bishop not blocking
Rook on same column as queen, bishop not blocking
Bishop on same diagonal, rook not blocking
Statement example 2 — rook and bishop both attack in 1
Rook on same row but bishop blocks between rook and queen
Bishop on same diagonal but rook blocks between bishop and queen
Neither piece can directly reach queen — answer 2

stress.description: All valid distinct-position 3-piece configurations on 8x8 board
